scope: 20 source files from .bun/install/cache — full lib/ directory + package.json

vulnerabilities.description: HTML parser faithfully processes ALL HTML features including script tags, iframe, event handler attributes (onerror, onload, onclick), and javascript: URLs. No sanitization layer exists between parsing and serialization. If consuming application parses untrusted HTML then serializes output to client without sanitizing, stored/reflected XSS is possible. The parser is spec-compliant — this is expected DOM behavior — but the risk to consuming applications is high.

outerHTML setter (unlike innerHTML which is utils.nyi stub) has partial implementation that calls mozHTMLParser to parse user-supplied HTML, then replaces node in DOM tree. This is the only HTML setter with a live implementation path. Inconsistent with innerHTML stub behavior — developers may test with innerHTML (no-op), assume safety, then deploy outerHTML (live parser) in production.

Location.assign(url) resolves and navigates to user-supplied URLs without protocol validation. javascript:, data:, vbscript: URLs pass through unchecked. No origin comparison or allowlist. If assign() is reachable from user-controlled input (redirect parameter, hash fragment), attacker can redirect to arbitrary origins or execute JavaScript in renderer context.

Library version 2.2.0 is outdated. Latest release is 2.3.x. Known CVE history for domino includes prototype pollution and parser bypass issues in prior versions. Run `bun audit` or `npm audit` to check current dependency tree for known vulnerabilities.

CustomEvent constructor copies ALL enumerable properties from user-supplied dictionary onto the event object via `for(var p in dictionary) { this[p] = dictionary[p]; }`. No property allowlist. Attacker can set __proto__, constructor, or other internal properties. While modern JS runtimes block __proto__ assignment on ordinary objects, this pattern is fragile and vulnerable to prototype pollution in older engines.

Proxy `has` trap returns `true` unconditionally — every property check succeeds. `'__proto__' in style`, `'constructor' in style`, `'toString' in style` all return true. Masks missing properties; could confuse security-sensitive feature detection or sandbox checks relying on `in` operator.

_dispatchEvent accesses `target._handlers[type]` via bracket notation with event.type as key. If type equals '__proto__', 'constructor', or 'toString', prototype pollution may occur. String-type coercion of event.type mitigates direct exploitation, but the pattern is fragile — no Object.create(null) guard on _handlers lookup.

Event constructor sets `this.timeStamp = Date.now()`. Exposes server-side system time to any code that creates or dispatches events. Minor information disclosure — attacker can determine server clock skew for timing attacks.

replaceData converts `count` via `>>> 0` (unsigned right shift) which transforms negative values into very large positive integers (e.g., -1 becomes 4294967295). If count originates from untrusted source (XPath expression, DOM manipulation from user input), this creates unbounded substring operation that could exhaust memory or cause denial of service.

getList() splits attribute value on whitespace regex without input length limit. Attacker-controlled class attribute with extremely long whitespace-delimited token string (e.g., megabyte-scale) could cause CPU exhaustion in split() and subsequent filter() operations. Add max-length guard before split.

traverse() method iterates DOM via `elt.nextElement(this.root)` with NO iteration counter or safety limit. If DOM tree becomes cyclic due to corruption or other bugs, infinite loop results. Add maxIterations guard (e.g., 100,000 nodes) to prevent hang.

innerHTML and outerHTML setters are both stubbed to `utils.nyi` (no-op). Contrasts with Element.js where outerHTML has a live parser-backed setter. Inconsistent API surface — developers testing innerHTML assignment on DocumentFragment will see silent no-op behavior, potentially masking XSS bugs that manifest only when ported to Element.outerHTML.

Event constructor and initEvent provide two initialization paths with subtly different flag handling. Constructor sets _initialized=true and _dispatching=false. initEvent resets these plus _propagationStopped, _immediatePropagationStopped, defaultPrevented, isTrusted. CustomEvent delegates to Event constructor then applies dictionary properties. Two initialization paths increase attack surface for subclass confusion.

hardcodedSecrets.note: No credential surface — pure DOM library with zero network/auth code

sqlInjection.note: No database interaction — library operates on in-memory DOM trees only

xssVectors.note: HTML parser faithfully preserves unsafe content; outerHTML setter live-parses; innerHTML stub creates false safety signal

authenticationAuthorization.note: No auth mechanism — server-side DOM has no concept of users/sessions

dependencyRisk.note: Version 2.2.0 outdated; known CVE history for prototype pollution and parser bypass

urlProtocolSafety.note: Location.assign() and URL.js expose user/pass properties without protocol allowlisting or SSRF guard (no network I/O = limited practical impact)

notFound: No hardcoded API keys, passwords, tokens, or secrets in library source
No SQL queries, database connections, or ORM usage
No eval(), Function(), setTimeout(), setInterval(), or fetch() calls
No file system access (fs, path, require.resolve with user input)
No ReDoS-vulnerable regex patterns (nested quantifiers like (a+)+$)
No child_process, exec, or spawn calls
No crypto operations with hardcoded keys/IVs
No prototype pollution via __proto__ assignment in property copy loops (defensive Object.create(null) used in 105+ locations)
positiveFindings: Object.create(null) used consistently (105+ locations) to block prototype pollution on dictionaries, ID maps, listener stores, and attribute maps
NodeUtils.js escape() and escapeAttr() functions encode <, >, &, ",   as HTML entities during serialization
escapeMatchingClosingTag() prevents script/style content injection by escaping </ sequences in raw text elements
HTMLParser implements full HTML5 tokenizer with script_data_state for safe script tag content parsing
Test suite includes 29 XSS test cases (test/xss.js) validating mutation-based XSS vectors per Cure53 research
Template element isolates content in separate DocumentFragment (template context document)
insertAdjacentHTML validates context element — blocks insertion when context is Document or html root
Script elements have NO execution mechanism — text setter creates TextNode, no runScript() hook exists
innerHTML/outerHTML getters use proper serialization with entity encoding, preventing XSS on read path
CSSStyleDeclaration._parsed cache keyed on cssText comparison with _lastParsedText, preventing stale attribute reflection
recommendations: CRITICAL: If application parses untrusted HTML via domino, ALWAYS sanitize output with DOMPurify (server-side) or equivalent before sending to client — the parser preserves all scripts, event handlers, and javascript: URLs by design.
Upgrade domino to latest 2.3.x release: `bun update @mixmark-io/domino`. Check changelog for security fixes between 2.2.0 and current.
Run `bun audit` or `npm audit` to check dependency tree for known CVEs, including transitive dependencies.
Validate URL protocols before passing to Location.assign() — reject javascript:, data:, vbscript: schemes. Implement origin allowlist for redirect targets.
Do not rely on Element.innerHTML setter for sanitization — it is a no-op stub. Implement explicit HTML sanitization pipeline if application sets innerHTML/outerHTML from user input.
Consider replacing domino with actively maintained alternative (linkedom, happy-dom) that has current security patching, full innerHTML support, and larger test coverage.
Add Content-Security-Policy headers with script-src 'self' and object-src 'none' in any application consuming this library's serialized output.
Add max-length guard on DOMTokenList.getList() input (e.g., 64KB) before regex split to prevent CPU exhaustion from oversized class attributes.
Add iteration cap (100,000 nodes) to FilteredElementList.traverse() to prevent infinite loops on corrupted DOM trees.
Validate offset/count parameters in CharacterData.replaceData to reject negative count values before >>> 0 conversion.
Audit all code paths where event.type, CSS property names, or DOM attribute names derive from user input — validate against allowlists before passing to domino APIs.
Application-layer: never expose domino directly to user-supplied HTML. Always parse, sanitize, then serialize in a controlled pipeline with CSP headers on the output.
